Title: The OsmiR396–OsGRF8–OsF3H-flavonoid pathway mediates resistance to the brown planthopper in rice

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed hulls; leaves/seedlings (total flavonoid assay in fresh leaves)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances rice resistance to brown planthopper (BPH); associated with insect antibiosis/selection and tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: pre-OsmiR396a and pre-OsmiR396b induced by BPH as early as 2 h, peaking at 8 h; OsmiR396b also induced by salt
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: BPH-responsive in rice seedlings/leaf sheaths; induced under salt treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Brown planthopper infestation; 100 mM NaCl salt treatment; exogenous flavonoid treatment
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Target mimicry construct MIM396; promoter not stated in this paper
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsGRF8; other OsGRFs as miR396 targets
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Target mimicry sequestration in transgenic rice across three genetic backgrounds; BPH resistance assays; honeydew assay; BPH weight gain assay; tolerance test; flavonoid quantification; Arabidopsis MIM396 transformation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of flavonoid accumulation and BPH resistance; sequestration of OsmiR396 increases flavonoids and resistance to BPH and salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsmiR396 is induced by BPH infestation and salt treatment in rice. 2. Sequestering OsmiR396 by MIM396 increases flavonoid content in rice and anthocyanin in Arabidopsis. 3. MIM396 plants show enhanced BPH resistance across multiple rice backgrounds, with reduced BPH weight gain and honeydew excretion. 4. Genetic evidence places OsmiR396 upstream of OsGRF8 and OsF3H in a flavonoid-mediated resistance pathway.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No visible differences in plant height or tiller number in MIM396 plants; discussion suggests additional beneficial effects on grain size/seed quality rather than penalties
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Sequestering OsmiR396 is a promising breeding strategy to simultaneously enhance BPH resistance, salt tolerance, and potentially grain/seed quality
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mature OsmiR396a and OsmiR396b share the same sequence; authors focused mainly on OsmiR396b response but functional mimic likely affects the miR396 family

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core early flavonoid pathway leading to dihydroflavonols/anthocyanin-related flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step not firmly established; presented as an important BPH-responsive flavonoid biosynthetic gene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rice tissues assayed for total flavonoids; increased pigmentation in Arabidopsis stems/leaves/siliques upon overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to resistance against brown planthopper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct intermediate required for downstream flavonoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in MIM396 background and activated by OsGRF8; expression altered in overexpression and RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Previous microarray evidence cited for BPH induction; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression; RNAi construct promoter not clearly stated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and RNAi in rice; BPH resistance assays; host choice assay; flavonoid quantification; Arabidopsis overexpression; genetic complementation by crossing OsF3HR16 with MIM396
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates flavonoid accumulation and enhances BPH resistance; RNAi reduces both flavonoids and resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsF3H is a BPH-responsive flavonoid biosynthetic gene upregulated in MIM396 plants. 2. Overexpression of OsF3H increases rice flavonoid content and BPH resistance, whereas RNAi has opposite effects. 3. OsF3H overexpression in Arabidopsis increases anthocyanin accumulation. 4. Downregulation of OsF3H in the MIM396 background complements the elevated flavonoid content and enhanced BPH resistance, placing OsF3H downstream of OsmiR396–OsGRF8.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering flavonoid accumulation and insect resistance; may be stacked with upstream regulators such as miR396/GRF8
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsF3H-mediated resistance appeared to involve a host selection/settling mechanism in BPH assays

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRF (Growth-Regulating Factor) family transcription factor; binds CGC(G)A(C)G(A)-type motifs
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rice tissues assayed for total flavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in MIM396 plants; induced at 6 h after BPH infestation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: BPH-responsive and elevated in GRF8OE plants
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Negatively regulated by OsmiR396; induced during BPH infestation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsF3H transcription by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-responsive positive regulator connecting miR396 signaling to flavonoid accumulation and BPH resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in rice; BPH resistance assay; flavonoid measurement; yeast one-hybrid; ChIP-qPCR; Dual-LUC transient assay in tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of flavonoid accumulation and BPH resistance through direct activation of OsF3H
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGRF8 is a miR396 target whose transcript accumulates in MIM396 plants and is induced by BPH infestation. 2. OsGRF8 overexpression enhances BPH resistance and increases flavonoid contents. 3. OsGRF8 directly binds motifs in the OsF3H promoter and activates OsF3H transcription. 4. OsGRF8 therefore links OsmiR396 repression to downstream flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising positive regulator for engineering insect resistance by boosting endogenous flavonoid biosynthesis; may be used with miR396 editing/sequestration strategies
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among 12 OsGRF miR396 targets, OsGRF8 was selected for detailed study; OsGRF4 was also BPH-induced but not functionally dissected here
